description: Drive engine MUST run as an isolated process. Communication is one-way: drive engine emits drive sensor values, main process never writes into drive engine state.
Drive rules live in PostgreSQL, NOT in the drive engine process memory. Drive engine reads rules from PostgreSQL at evaluation time.
Three Neo4j instances must remain fully separate: WKG (world knowledge, port 7687), KG(Self) (port 7690), KG(Other) (port 7689). No cross-instance queries.
TimescaleDB is the event backbone. All subsystem events (tick_event, context_event, response_event) must be written to TimescaleDB for durability and audit.
Type 1 (reflex) actions must convince the reasoning engine they are better than Type 2 (deliberate) before executing. Type 1 never bypasses reasoning.
The executor engine is the central cognitive loop. Every tick processes inputs, runs predictions, selects an action, and emits a tick_event to TimescaleDB and drive engine.
Learning pipeline reads events with hasLearned=false from TimescaleDB, extracts entities, upserts to WKG, extracts edges, then marks events as learned. This is the only path for new knowledge.
Text input must carry speaker identity (person entity) before entering encoders or episodic memory. Anonymous text must not be stored as attributed knowledge.
Self-evaluation reads from KG(Self) only. It evaluates predictions against outcomes using the self-knowledge graph, not the world knowledge graph.
Other evaluation (person modeling) reads/writes KG(Other) only. Person models are stored separately from world knowledge and self-knowledge.
Planning subsystem processes opportunities detected by the drive engine. It researches, simulates, proposes plans, and validates via LLM constraints. Plans become procedures only after validation.
Never use __dirname or hardcoded absolute paths to reference repo-root files. Use process.cwd() for runtime path resolution.
Wire pipelines end-to-end. Do not leave zero-vector placeholders or stub implementations silently. If something is not yet implemented, it must be explicitly flagged.
Failed predictions from the drive engine feed back to decision-making, giving more weight to Type 2 deliberation. This feedback loop must be preserved.
World Knowledge Graph edges should use ACT-R inspired confidence dynamics. Edge weights decay over time and strengthen with repeated activation.
Frontend is a read-only dashboard and chat interface. It must not directly write to any database. All mutations flow through the NestJS backend API.
The drive engine computes exactly 12 drives. Adding or removing drives requires architectural review. Each drive has a rule set in PostgreSQL and a sensor value emitted per tick.
Episodic memory is read by the inner monologue to inform the executor engine. It must contain timestamped experiential records, not just factual knowledge.
